steps.ordered: Ultrasound Abdomen (LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN))
CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

study_inadequacy.evidence: The body and tail obscured by overlying bowel gas.

alarm_detect.summary: The abdominal ultrasound did not fully visualize the pancreas — the body and tail were obscured by bowel gas — leaving the pancreatic question unanswered despite a normal lipase, and further imaging (CT) may be needed to evaluate the full pancreas and right-sided structures.

alarm_resolve.reason: CT abdomen and pelvis with contrast is fully capable of visualizing the pancreatic body and tail that were obscured by bowel gas on ultrasound, and while the physician's stated reasoning focuses on other unexplained findings, surveying the 'entire abdomen' inherently encompasses complete pancreatic evaluation, satisfying the study inadequacy flag.
